title: neonauclea jagorii
intro: Neonauclea jagorii, also known as the Jagor's Neonauclea, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. It is a type of plant in the family Rubiaceae, which is known for its medicinal properties. Neonauclea jagorii contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against infections. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
